PRIVATE & CONFIDENTIAL
FUND TRANSFER AND INVESTMENT PARTICIPATION AGREEMENT
Agreement No.: PAL-ROY-500M-2026 Reference Code: MX-500M-ROY-2026 Date: June 24, 2026

This Agreement is made for the purpose of the direct transfer of funds totaling 500,000,000 USD (five hundred million US Dollars) via the ISO 20022 MX format (pacs.008/pacs.009) and the use of these funds for specific real estate investment projects.

1. Parties

This party is referred to as Royal Invest GmbH or the "Fund Recipient."

2. Recitals (Whereas)
  • PALMAVB INVEST, LLP wishes to transfer its funds held at DEUTSCHE BANK AG to the designated account of Royal Invest GmbH.
  • Royal Invest GmbH is ready, willing, and able to receive these funds in ISO 20022 MX (pacs.008/pacs.009) format and to credit them to the relevant bank account.
  • PALMAVB INVEST, LLP warrants that the funds to be transferred are good, clean, free, of non-criminal origin, and free and clear of any liens, encumbrances, or third-party claims.
  • Royal Invest GmbH undertakes that the funds will only be invested in the real estate projects located in Hessen, as listed in Annex-1, and will not be used for any other purpose.
  • Royal Invest GmbH accepts the participation conditions (profit/loss sharing, term, termination, penalty, etc.) set out below regarding the use of these funds.
  • The Parties sign this Agreement both to execute the transfer and to establish the principles for the investment use of the funds.
3. Definitions
  • "Agreement": This document and its annexes.
  • "MX Format": ISO 20022 compliant pacs.008 and/or pacs.009 messages.
  • "Total Amount": 500,000,000 USD (five hundred million US Dollars).
  • "First Tranche": 50,000,000 USD.
  • "Second Tranche": 55,000,000 USD.
  • "Third Tranche": 55,000,000 USD.
  • "Fourth Tranche": 55,000,000 USD.
  • "Fifth Tranche": 55,000,000 USD.
  • "Sixth and Subsequent Tranches": 230,000,000 USD – to be separately agreed.
  • "Project": The purchase, development, management, and sale of the real estate properties specified in Annex-1.
  • "PGL": Payment Guarantee Letter, an irrevocable undertaking issued by the Recipient's bank upon confirmation of funds.
  • "Business Day": A day on which banks are open in Germany or the United Kingdom.
  • "UETR": Unique End-to-End Transaction Reference.
4. Transfer Conditions (MX Procedure)
4.1. Closing Procedures
  1. Both Parties shall execute, sign, and seal this Agreement. It is explicitly stated that the transfer will be carried out via the MX format.
  2. Within 24 business hours of receiving the signed Agreement from PALMAVB INVEST, LLP, Royal Invest GmbH shall conduct due diligence, establish direct communication with PALMAVB INVEST, LLP or its legal representative, and only then countersign and return the Agreement. At this stage, the Agreement becomes binding.
  3. Both Parties shall deposit a copy of this Agreement with their respective bank officers.
  4. Within 48 business hours of the mutual signing of this Agreement, PALMAVB INVEST, LLP shall transfer the first tranche of 50,000,000 USD to Royal Invest GmbH's bank account via the MX format (pacs.008/pacs.009).
  5. Within 48 business hours of receiving the MX transfer receipt, Royal Invest GmbH's bank shall credit the funds to Royal Invest GmbH's account. A copy of the successful MX transfer message shall be sent to Royal Invest GmbH.
  6. The second, third, fourth, and fifth tranches (55,000,000 USD each) and the remaining 230,000,000 USD shall be executed in accordance with clauses 4 and 5 above, at times separately agreed upon by the Parties.
4.2. Detailed Operational Steps (Enhanced)
  1. Step 1 – Execution: Parties sign the Agreement and exchange scanned copies via official email.
  2. Step 2 – Readiness Confirmation: Within 48 banking hours after signing, the Funding Party's bank officer sends an email to the Recipient's bank officer with a copy of the Agreement and a letter confirming readiness to commence.
  3. Step 3 – Response: Within 24 banking hours after receiving the readiness confirmation, the Recipient's bank officer responds with a letter of readiness to begin work.
  4. Step 4 – Transfer & Notification: Within 48 banking hours after receiving the response, the Funding Party initiates the transfer. The Funding Party's bank officer sends the CAMT.054 (bank-to-customer debit/credit notification) and a screenshot of the Black System screen showing transfer details (account, beneficiary, amount, ID/transaction codes).
  5. Step 5 – Verification & PGL: Within 72 banking hours after receiving the SWIFT copies and screenshots, the Recipient's bank officer verifies the submitted copies. If the funds have arrived and are recorded in the Nostro/Common Account, the Recipient issues a Payment Guarantee Letter (PGL) equal to the amount of the tranche received. The PGL is an integral part of this Agreement.
  6. Step 6 – Redistribution: After successful loading of the funds and verification of the PGL by the receiving banks, the Recipient's bank officer is obligated to redistribute the funds for reinvestment purposes within 72 banking hours, in accordance with the PGL.
  7. Step 7 – Failure: In the event of failure by the Recipient to fulfill its obligations, the Funding Party has the right to unilaterally terminate the Agreement and block or cancel the payment.

Note: The operation is considered complete upon confirmed receipt of funds. Telephone or on-screen confirmation is not provided. The Recipient's bank employee is not to contact the Funding Party's bank employee directly.

4.3. Transaction Instrument Details
  • Format: ISO 20022 MX (pacs.008/pacs.009)
  • Type: Cash credit to Nostro account
  • Total Face Value: 500,000,000 USD (with rollovers and extensions)
  • Purpose: Real estate investment project as per Annex-1
  • First Tranche: 35,000,000 USD
  • Second Tranche: 55,000,000 USD
  • Third Tranche: 55,000,000 USD
  • Fourth Tranche: 55,000,000 USD
  • Fifth Tranche: 70,000,000 USD
  • Sixth and Subsequent: 230,000,000 USD (to be agreed)
4.4. Payment and Settlement
  • Payment Method: Wire Transfer (or USDT where mutually agreed) within 5 banking days upon issuance of the CAMT.054.
  • Final Settlement: Upon successful processing and final settlement of the relevant MX messages, the Recipient Bank's Nostro account shall be irrevocably and unconditionally credited with good and cleared funds within a settlement window of T+0 to T+72 hours, subject to standard clearing cycles, compliance screening, and applicable AML/CTF requirements.
  • Royal Invest GmbH's bank shall, upon final settlement, post the corresponding credit to the customer account without undue delay.
4.5. Operational / Technical Requirements
  • Royal Invest GmbH shall ensure that its duly authorized bank officer maintains active access to the SWIFT network.
  • All tracking, monitoring, and reconciliation activities shall be performed using the UETR.
  • All interbank operational communications shall be conducted exclusively through authenticated banking channels.
  • Note: If the Recipient Bank maintains a direct correspondent banking relationship with Deutsche Bank AG, ISO 20022 pacs.009 shall not be applicable.
5. Investment Participation Conditions

Royal Invest GmbH shall utilize the funds in the real estate projects specified in Annex-1 according to the following principles.

5.1. Participation and Investment Purpose
  • The funds shall be used for the joint acquisition, management, leasing, and subsequent sale of the specified real estate properties in Hessen.
  • Royal Invest GmbH shall spend these funds exclusively on the projects listed in Annex-1.
5.2. Use of Investment Funds

The funds may be used for: Purchase price, Notary and land registry fees, Real estate transfer tax, Brokerage fees, Renovation and refurbishment costs, Ongoing management and operating costs, Construction (superstructure and infrastructure) costs.

5.3. Profit and Loss Sharing
  • Profits and losses shall be shared proportionally according to the total amount invested by Royal Invest GmbH.
  • Profit components: Net rental income, sales proceeds, and other income.
  • Deductions before distribution: Financing costs, maintenance/repair costs, management costs, reserve fund formation, taxes and duties.
5.4. Term and Termination
  • This Agreement is concluded for a term of 10 years.
  • Ordinary termination is not possible within the first 10 years. At the end of the 10th year, termination is possible with a notice period of at least 12 months.
  • The right to extraordinary termination for good cause remains reserved.
5.5. Penalty Clause
  • If Royal Invest GmbH terminates the Agreement prematurely or demands the return of a portion of the funds, it agrees to pay a lump-sum compensation of 50% of the prematurely requested amount.
5.6. Confidentiality
  • The Parties are obliged to keep all information strictly confidential. Information may not be shared with employees, consultants, affiliates, or other third parties.
  • The confidentiality obligation survives the termination of this Agreement.
5.7. Tax Provisions / Supervision / Effectiveness
  • Each Party is solely responsible for the proper taxation of its own income.
  • The funds provided are not subject to any state control or administrative supervision.
  • This Agreement shall enter into force on the date it is signed and the first tranche is paid.
6. Claims of the Parties

In case of any questions or claims related to sending, installation or discrepancy of the final codes from the Recipient, the Recipient is obliged to provide a letter describing the problem on the company letterhead with the signature of the first manager and the seal. The Recipient is also obliged to attach a screenshot of the bank employee's screen to the letter as evidence of the discrepancy. All these documents must be sent from the Recipient's bank employee's email to the Funding Party's bank employee's email, and a copy must be provided to the Funding Party's representatives. In case of the Recipient's refusal to provide any evidence, the Funding Party's obligations will be considered fulfilled and the Funding Party reserves the right to block the payment for further investigation.

7. Non-Solicitation

The Recipient hereby confirms and declares that the Funding Party, its associates or representatives or any person or persons on its behalf has/have never been solicited by any party in any way whatsoever that can be construed as a solicitation for this transaction. Any delay in or failure of performance by either party shall constitute a breach. The term "Beyond the Control of Such Party" includes Act of War, Rebellion, Fire, Flood, Earthquake or other natural disasters.

8. Representations and Warranties
  • Organization: Each party is duly organized, validly existing and in good standing.
  • Enforceability: This Agreement constitutes the legal, valid and binding obligation of such party.
  • Consents and Authority: No consents or approvals are required from any governmental authority.
  • No Conflict: The execution does not conflict with any organizational documents or laws.
9. Miscellaneous
  • Notices: Any modifications, amendments, or addendums will be executed by the two authorized signatories respectively. Facsimile or photocopies certified as true copies shall be considered as an original.
  • Specific Performance: The Parties recognize rights are unique and may seek injunctive relief.
  • Entire Agreement: This Agreement supersedes all prior agreements.
  • Amendments: This Agreement may not be amended except by an instrument in writing signed by both Parties.
  • Severability: If any provision is invalid, the remainder shall continue in full force and effect.
  • Counterparts: This Agreement may be executed in one or more counterparts, all considered one and the same.
  • No Third-Party Rights: This Agreement is solely for the benefit of the Parties.
  • Headings: Headings are for convenience only and shall not control.
10. Waiver of Jury Trial

The Parties hereto hereby irrevocably and unconditionally waive trial by jury in any legal action or proceeding relating to this Agreement.

11. Governing Law and Arbitration
  • This Agreement shall be governed by and interpreted in accordance with German substantive law (BGB).
  • All disputes shall be finally settled by arbitration in accordance with the arbitration rules of the Frankfurt am Main Chamber of Commerce (IHK Frankfurt am Main).
  • The place of arbitration shall be Frankfurt am Main, and the language of arbitration shall be German.
  • The ordinary courts are excluded.

ANNEX-3 – REAL ESTATE INVESTMENT PARTICIPATION AGREEMENT (BETEILIGUNGSVERTRAG)

This Annex is an integral part of the main Agreement. The additional conditions regarding the use of the funds are set out below.

Preamble: The Funding Party (PALMAVB INVEST, LLP) provides a total participation capital of 500,000,000 USD for the financing, structuring, and acquisition of the real estate portfolio in Hessen as listed in Annex-1.

  1. Participation and Contributions: To be paid to Royal Invest GmbH, IBAN: DE38 5019 0000 6000 2401 90, BIC: FFVBDEFFXXX.
  2. Financing: Parties cooperate in financing according to participation quotas.
  3. Use of Funds: Purchase, notary, taxes, renovation, management, construction.
  4. Profit/Loss: Distributed according to profit quota.
  5. Term: 10 years, termination after 10 years with 12 months' notice.
  6. Penalty: 50% of prematurely requested capital.
  7. Confidentiality: Strictly confidential, survives termination.
  8. Tax & Supervision: Each party responsible; no state control.
  9. Amendments: Must be in writing.
  10. Severability: Invalid provisions do not affect the rest.
  11. Jurisdiction: Frankfurt IHK Arbitration, German law.

Electronic Document Transmissions (EDT)

EDT's shall be deemed valid and enforceable in respect of any provisions of this Contract. As applicable, this agreement shall incorporate U.S. Public Law 106-229, "Electronic Signatures in Global and National Commerce Act" or such other applicable law conforming to the UNCITRAL Model Law on Electronic Signatures (2001) and the ELECTRONIC COMMERCE AGREEMENT (ECE/TRADE/257, Geneva, May 2000) adopted by the United Nations Centre for Trade Facilitation and Electronic Business (UN/CEFACT). EDT documents shall be subject to European Community Directive No. 95/46/EEC, as applicable. Either Party may request hard copy of any document that has been previously transmitted by electronic means provided however, that any such request shall in no manner delay the parties from performing their respective obligations.

Electronic signature is valid and legally binding as an original if transmitted in secure and certified *.pdf format.

*** THE END OF THE AGREEMENT ***
This Agreement is a single, valid, and binding document that reflects the Parties' intent, combining ISO 20022 MX transfer provisions with real estate participation conditions.
